facts: The patient's name is Gregory.
The patient has a nonhealing foot ulcer.
The foot ulcer has lasted two months.
The patient's foot ulcer has recently become red.
The patient's foot ulcer is draining.
The patient is concerned about the healing of the foot ulcer.
The patient thinks they may have gotten the foot ulcer from walking barefoot on the beach.
The patient saw their PCP before coming in.
The PCP prescribed medication for the patient.
The patient did not pick up the prescribed medication from the pharmacy.
The patient does not recall the name of the prescribed medication.
The patient has taken the medication before.
The patient went to Dewey Beach when they hurt their foot.
The patient works as an EMT.
The patient's foot sometimes throbs.
The patient does not have specific pain.
The patient's foot ulcer started to drain.
The patient's foot ulcer looks red.
The patient has not had drainage from the foot ulcer before.
The patient has diabetes.
The patient takes medication for diabetes.
The patient does not check their sugar levels all the time.
The patient is not sure how to check their sugar levels.
The patient's last A1c level was around 10.
The doctor performed a physical exam on the patient's foot.
The patient's vital signs are normal.
The patient does not have a fever.
The patient has a 1 by 2 inch circular wound on the dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot.
The wound is proximal to the fifth MTP joint.
The patient has redness around the wound.
The patient has drainage present around the wound.
The patient has edema around the wound.
The patient has fluid coming out of the wound.
The patient does not have necrosis.
The patient does not have any odor.
The patient does not have any bony exposure.
The doctor thinks the patient has an infection.
The patient feels increased pressure when the wound is touched.
The patient does not have distinct pain when the wound is touched.
The patient had an x-ray of their right foot.
The x-ray showed no evidence of osteomyelitis.
The doctor ordered an ABI (ankle brachial index) for the patient.
The ABI is to determine the blood supply in the patient's foot.
The doctor performed a debridement on the patient's foot.
The doctor prescribed clindamycin for the patient.
Clindamycin is 400 milligrams.
Clindamycin is taken twice a day.
Clindamycin is taken for 7 days.
Clindamycin is prescribed for the infection.
The patient may be given a surgical shoe.
The surgical shoe is to reduce pressure on the foot.
The patient is to wear the surgical shoe for the next month.
The doctor wants to see the patient back in two weeks.
The doctor may perform another debridement in two weeks.
The patient can call the office if they have any issues.
The patient should go to the urgent care or ER if they have a fever or other emergency symptoms.
